purpose: Evaluate and integrate n8n as a policy-gated external workflow/integration worker for Ananta. n8n should be used for connector-heavy, webhook-driven and low/medium-risk automation flows while Ananta Hub remains the owner of policy, approval, state, audit, task planning and artifact verification.
design_principles: Ananta Hub owns policy, approval, audit, task state, artifact contracts and orchestration.
n8n is an external workflow execution adapter, not an autonomous Ananta planner and not a replacement for native workers.
All n8n workflow execution must be capability-gated and policy-checked by the Hub before invocation.
Default mode is dry-run/read-only/sandboxed where possible.
Secrets and credentials must stay behind explicit boundaries and must not be copied into task prompts, logs or artifacts.
n8n results must return as structured Ananta artifacts/results with provenance, status and redaction.
No n8n workflow may bypass Ananta approval, release gates or worker/tool policy.
non_goals: Do not replace Ananta Hub orchestration with n8n workflow logic.
Do not let n8n decide task priority, approval, security level or autonomous code changes.
Do not expose unrestricted n8n credentials to Ananta workers or LLM prompts.
Do not auto-enable write/admin n8n workflows by default.
Do not require n8n for basic Ananta local-dev usage.
Do not make n8n a hard dependency of the core OSS golden path.
success_criteria: Ananta can register n8n as an optional external workflow backend/worker adapter.
The Hub can list allowed n8n workflows with declared capabilities, risk class, input schema and output artifact schema.
The Hub can start a policy-approved n8n workflow and correlate the callback/result to an Ananta task/trace.
n8n execution results are stored as Ananta ResultArtifact/IntegrationRunArtifact-like outputs with provenance.
Dry-run/test mode works without real external credentials.
Write/admin workflows require explicit capability and approval.
Secrets are redacted in logs, task history and artifacts.
Docs clearly distinguish n8n learning/UX patterns from n8n-as-worker integration.
recommended_use_cases: Slack/Matrix/Telegram/email notifications after task state changes.
GitHub/Jira/Confluence issue or comment workflows after explicit approval.
Webhook receiver/forwarder flows for external systems.
Low-risk data synchronization and report delivery.
Periodic status digest generation from already-approved Ananta artifacts.
Human-in-the-loop integration steps such as requesting external approval or notifying reviewers.
forbidden_or_deferred_use_cases: Auto-merge, auto-push or direct production mutation without Ananta approval.
Credential discovery or credential exfiltration flows.
Letting n8n invoke arbitrary Ananta tools without Hub-issued scoped token/capability.
Using n8n as the source of truth for Ananta task/plan state.

tasks.title: n8n integration boundary ADR add
acceptance_criteria: ADR defines n8n as optional external workflow/integration worker.
ADR states that Ananta Hub remains owner of policy, state, approval, audit and artifact verification.
ADR defines read-only/dry-run-first integration stance.
ADR lists forbidden uses such as auto-merge, unrestricted shell and credential exfiltration.
ADR explains relation to MCP/external tool integration boundaries.

tasks.title: n8n UX learnings document add
acceptance_criteria: Document captures useful n8n patterns: visible execution history, node/connector model, retry paths, trigger model, credential UX and manual approval nodes.
Document explicitly avoids copying n8n as Ananta's core architecture.
Document maps learnings to Ananta surfaces: Hub, task board, artifact view, TUI/Web, integration registry.

tasks.title: n8n optional runtime profile add
acceptance_criteria: n8n is represented as optional integration runtime profile, not required local-dev dependency.
ananta init can mention or configure n8n endpoint only when user selects external workflow integration.
Docker/Compose n8n path is optional and documented separately.
No n8n credential is required for normal Ananta CLI usage.

tasks.title: n8n workflow descriptor schema add
acceptance_criteria: Schema describes workflow_id, display_name, description, trigger_url or workflow_ref, input_schema, output_schema, capability, risk_class, default_mode and approval_required.
Schema distinguishes read, write and admin workflow classes.
Schema supports dry_run_supported and callback_required flags.
Schema includes secret_ref names but not secret values.
Tests validate minimal read-only descriptor and full write workflow descriptor.

tasks.title: n8n workflow registry service add
acceptance_criteria: Registry loads workflow descriptors from config/integrations/n8n_workflows.json or injected test fixture.
Unknown workflows are unavailable by default.
Registry returns capability, risk_class, approval_required, dry_run_supported and input/output schema metadata.
Registry exposes degraded/disabled state when n8n endpoint is not configured.
Tests cover valid descriptors, unknown workflow, invalid descriptor and deterministic ordering.

tasks.title: n8n capabilities map to Ananta policy add
acceptance_criteria: Adapter maps workflow descriptor capability/risk/write class to existing Hub policy decision flow.
Read-only workflows can be allowed by policy without approval only when configured safe.
Write/admin workflows require explicit capability and approval according to policy.
Policy adapter cannot weaken existing Hub policy or approval gates.
Tests cover allow, confirm-required and blocked decisions.

tasks.title: n8n execution request model add
acceptance_criteria: Request model includes workflow_id, task_id, goal_id, trace_id, input payload, dry_run and requested_by.
Model validates input against descriptor schema where feasible.
Model rejects unknown workflow_id before execution.
Model carries correlation_id for callback/result matching.
Tests cover valid request, unknown workflow and invalid payload.

tasks.title: n8n execution adapter add
acceptance_criteria: Adapter invokes n8n webhook/workflow endpoint only after registry and policy checks pass.
Adapter supports dry-run mode that does not call real external n8n by default in tests.
Adapter attaches correlation_id, task_id, goal_id and trace_id to requests.
Adapter handles timeout, non-2xx response and malformed response as structured degraded result.
Adapter redacts secrets before logging or returning errors.
Tests use mocked HTTP client and do not require live n8n.

tasks.title: n8n callback receiver add
acceptance_criteria: Callback route accepts n8n result payloads with correlation_id.
Callback verifies shared secret/signature or scoped token.
Callback rejects unknown correlation_id and invalid auth.
Callback stores result as structured integration result/artifact or task history entry according to existing artifact flow.
Tests cover accepted callback, invalid auth, unknown correlation and redaction.

tasks.title: n8n IntegrationRunArtifact mapping add
acceptance_criteria: Artifact schema captures workflow_id, workflow_name, correlation_id, input_summary, output_summary, status, timestamps, external_run_ref and provenance.
Artifact never stores secret values.
Artifact records dry_run/read/write/admin class and approval reference when applicable.
Tests validate success, failed and dry-run artifact examples.

tasks.title: n8n credential boundary document add
acceptance_criteria: Document explains that n8n credentials remain in n8n or configured secret store, not in prompts or task payloads.
Document defines secret_ref usage and redaction requirements.
Document lists unsafe patterns that must be blocked.
Document explains least-privilege scoped webhook/token setup.
Document covers local-dev vs team/strict deployment differences.

tasks.title: n8n audit event coverage add
acceptance_criteria: Audit logs record workflow requested, policy decision, execution started, callback received, result stored and failure/degraded states.
Audit records include task_id, goal_id, trace_id, workflow_id and correlation_id.
Audit records do not include secret values or raw credential-bearing payloads.
Tests verify audit coverage for success and blocked execution.

tasks.title: n8n outbound token and signature verification add
acceptance_criteria: Provides helper for generating/verifying scoped n8n callback tokens or HMAC signatures.
Tokens/signatures are bound to correlation_id or workflow_id where feasible.
Replay protection is documented or implemented with timestamp/nonce if feasible.
Tests cover valid signature, invalid signature, expired timestamp and wrong correlation_id.

tasks.title: n8n notification workflow dogfood descriptor add
acceptance_criteria: Example descriptor defines a low-risk notification workflow such as task status digest or artifact-ready notification.
Example is dry-run/read-only by default.
Docs explain how to map the descriptor to an actual n8n webhook URL.
No real credentials are included.

tasks.title: n8n GitHub/Jira comment workflow design add
acceptance_criteria: Design explains how approved ReviewArtifacts could be posted to GitHub/Jira through n8n.
Design keeps outbound posting separate from code mutation and auto-merge.
Design requires redaction and approval for write workflows.
Implementation remains deferred until core n8n adapter and PR-review agent are stable.

tasks.title: n8n integration docs add
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain n8n as optional integration worker.
Docs show local-dev dry-run setup and optional real n8n endpoint setup.
Docs show workflow descriptor example and callback flow.
Docs explain security boundaries and what n8n must not do.
README only mentions n8n as optional integration, not core dependency.

tasks.title: n8n integration smoke and release-gate checks add
acceptance_criteria: Smoke test verifies registry load, policy block/allow path, dry-run execution and callback parsing without live n8n.
Release gate can include n8n smoke only when n8n integration runtime is claimed.
Smoke fails if execution adapter can call unknown workflow or bypass policy.
Smoke fails if secret-looking values appear in result artifacts or audit logs.

execution_order_summary: Start with the ADR and credential boundary so n8n cannot become a second uncontrolled automation plane.
Add workflow descriptor schema and registry before any execution adapter.
Wire policy adapter before live invocation.
Implement dry-run/mocked execution adapter first, then callback receiver and artifact mapping.
Add audit/signature/redaction tests before enabling write workflows.
Dogfood with a low-risk notification workflow before GitHub/Jira write workflows.
summary_notes: n8n is most useful for integrations, notifications, webhooks and external SaaS workflow glue.
Ananta should learn from n8n's visible execution history and connector UX, but must not copy n8n as its core architecture.
The first implementation should be dry-run and mock-friendly so tests do not require a live n8n instance.
Write/admin workflows must stay explicit, policy-gated and approval-bound.
This track pairs naturally with MCP/external tool policy work but should remain optional for OSS local-dev.
